source: WSET L3 Official Marking Keys (6 wines: Rustenberg Chardonnay, Ata Rangi Pinot Noir, Ca di Alte Pinot Grigio, Château Dutruch Bordeaux, Château Doisy-Daëne Barsac, 16 Stops Shiraz)

appearance.note: Clarity is assessed but clarity 'clear' is expected in healthy wines; turbidity earns a fault note rather than a separate mark

required_categories.standard_wine: 1 mark each for primary, secondary, and tertiary. Remaining 2 marks from any category.
required_categories.simple_wine_exception: For simple wines (e.g., basic Pinot Grigio, entry-level Shiraz): 1 mark for stating the wine is SIMPLE, remaining 4 marks for any valid primary aromas.

flavour_characteristics_rules.standard_wine: 1 mark for primary, 1 for secondary, 1 for tertiary flavour characteristic
flavour_characteristics_rules.simple_wine_exception: 1 mark for stating wine is SIMPLE, remaining 2 marks for any valid primary flavours

palate.note: Red wine has 1 extra mark for tannin = 10 palate marks vs 9 for white

alcohol_percentage_note: Each marking key includes the wine's actual alcohol % – used by assessor to calibrate alcohol scale assessment
